mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient model serving and intelligent scheduling. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for specific hardware configurations.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

llm-d --version

```

> Download the latest CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation by checking the version.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a managed Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are ready and operational.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the autoscaler configuration.
> Apply the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d --all

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete all llm-d deployments.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up your configurations and data before upgrading. This step involves draining nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-v0.5.1.yaml

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resources allocated to existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable or is overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity and the status of the Kubernetes API server.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for intelligent inference scheduling and improved autoscaling features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the Running state.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
